commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(cli): validate gateway-rpc --timeout
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Gateway-RPC based CLI commands accept `--timeout`, but invalid values (e.g. `--timeout nope`) get coerced to `NaN` and silently fall back to `10_000ms`.
- Why it matters: Users think they're overriding the timeout, but a typo can change behavior (and the fallback is inconsistent with the CLI default `30_000ms`).
- What changed: Validate `--timeout` in `callGatewayFromCli` and reject invalid values with a clear error; keep the intended default at `30s` when the option is missing.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to gateway request/transport semantics beyond CLI option validation; no refactors outside the CLI gateway-rpc helper.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: `callGatewayFromCli` used `Number(opts.timeout)` without validation; `callGateway` treats non-finite timeouts as “unset” and defaults to `10_000ms`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No CLI-side validation for the `--timeout` option; no test locking in invalid-argument handling.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): Unknown.
- Why this regressed now: Likely existed since the gateway-rpc CLI helper was introduced.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/gateway-rpc.timeout-option.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: invalid `--timeout` values are rejected and never reach `callGateway`; missing timeout defaults to `30_000ms`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: avoids needing a live gateway and directly verifies the CLI helper behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Invalid `--timeout` values now fail fast with an error (`--timeout must be a positive integer (milliseconds)`) instead of silently falling back to `10_000ms`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No) No
- New/changed network calls? (Yes/No) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No) No
- Data access scope changed? (Yes/No) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node.js CLI
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Run any command using `addGatewayClientOptions` / `callGatewayFromCli` (e.g. `openclaw system presence ...`).
2. Pass an invalid timeout value: `--timeout nope`.

### Expected

- CLI rejects invalid `--timeout` input.

### Actual

- CLI coerces to `NaN` and `callGateway` silently falls back to `10_000ms` (inconsistent with the option default `30_000ms`).

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Evidence (after):
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/cli/gateway-rpc.timeout-option.test.ts`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Ran the targeted unit test above; confirmed valid values pass through and invalid values throw.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Missing timeout uses `30_000ms` default.
  - Non-numeric timeout rejects.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full built `openclaw` dist CLI end-to-end invocation (not required for this localized helper fix).

AI-assisted: Yes (OpenClaw bughunt agent). Testing: targeted unit test only.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes (except invalid input now errors)
- Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No
- Migration needed? (Yes/No) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commit `4cf4fd02d9`.
- Files/config to restore: `src/cli/gateway-rpc.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: CLI commands that use gateway-rpc helper now exiting early when passed invalid `--timeout`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: scripts passing invalid `--timeout` values previously (silently) accepted will now fail.
  - Mitigation: error message is explicit; behavior matches other CLI timeout parsing patterns in the repo.
target.author: ruanrrn
